stocks.name: Butterfly Network
stocks.explanation: Shares surged after Midjourney unveiled a full-body ultrasound scanner built with 40 Butterfly ultrasound-on-chip modules. The five-year licensing and co-development agreement could generate up to $74 million in payments for Butterfly. This marks Midjourney’s first hardware initiative and validates Butterfly’s chip-based imaging platform for AI-driven diagnostics.

stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Wolfspeed rallied on a GE Aerospace MOU to supply 10 kV silicon carbide MOSFET die and co-develop high-voltage SiC modules for AI, aerospace, and defense markets. The company also launched fifth-generation SiC MOSFETs and 3.3 kV power module families targeting AI data centers and grid-scale renewables. With ~33% short interest, positive catalysts triggered significant short-covering.

stocks.name: Accenture plc
stocks.explanation: Accenture slashed its full-year revenue growth forecast to 3-4% from 3-5% after Q3 earnings, warning of a cumulative $400 million hit to EMEA operations from geopolitical disruption. The guidance cut, combined with Q4 revenue projections below Wall Street consensus, triggered the steepest single-day decline in over a year and dragged global IT services peers lower.

stocks.name: EPAM Systems
stocks.explanation: EPAM fell sharply in sympathy with Accenture’s weak guidance, which raised concerns about slowing enterprise IT spending across the outsourcing sector. As a major Eastern European-headquartered IT services provider, EPAM faces additional headwinds from geopolitical uncertainty in the region. The selloff reflects broader market fears that AI-driven automation is cannibalizing traditional consulting and outsourcing revenue.

stocks.name: Corning Inc.
stocks.explanation: Corning surged as investors priced in the company’s role as a critical physical infrastructure provider for AI data centers. GPU clusters require massive fiber-optic bandwidth, and Corning’s recent multiyear, multibillion-dollar supply agreements with major hyperscalers like Amazon have positioned it as an indispensable backbone supplier for the AI buildout.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo gained on renewed investor interest in B2B data and sales intelligence platforms as enterprise AI adoption accelerates demand for high-quality business data. The stock has been volatile amid ongoing speculation about strategic alternatives and potential acquisition interest from larger enterprise software players.

stocks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions
stocks.explanation: Cognizant fell in the Accenture-led IT services selloff, as the consulting giant’s weak guidance raised fears of a broader discretionary spending slowdown across the sector. Cognizant, which derives significant revenue from discretionary consulting and digital transformation projects, is viewed as particularly vulnerable to client budget cuts driven by geopolitical uncertainty and AI disruption.

stocks.name: Intel Corporation
stocks.explanation: Intel surged after President Trump claimed Apple has agreed to design and build chips using Intel’s 18A-P foundry process. While neither company has officially confirmed the deal, the announcement represents a potential landmark win for Intel’s foundry turnaround strategy. The global memory shortage is also driving renewed interest in domestic semiconductor manufacturing capacity.

stocks.name: Super Micro Computer
stocks.explanation: Super Micro rallied after raising its fiscal 2026 revenue outlook to at least $40 billion, up from $36 billion, driven by strong demand for its AI-optimized server platforms. The upgraded guidance signals that enterprise and hyperscaler appetite for GPU-dense infrastructure remains robust despite broader IT spending concerns elsewhere in the market.

stocks.name: Lattice Semiconductor
stocks.explanation: Lattice Semiconductor rose as the broader semiconductor sector rallied on AI infrastructure demand and the Apple-Intel manufacturing news. Lattice’s low-power FPGAs are increasingly used in edge AI, industrial automation, and automotive applications, positioning the company to benefit from AI deployment beyond the data center.

stocks.name: Infosys Limited
stocks.explanation: Infosys tumbled in the global IT services selloff triggered by Accenture’s weak guidance, with its US-listed shares dropping over 10%. As India’s second-largest IT exporter, Infosys is a bellwether for the outsourcing sector. Indian IT stocks have now shed nearly 30% of their market value this year amid fears that AI automation is structurally undermining the traditional offshoring model.

stocks.name: Quantum Computing Inc.
stocks.explanation: Quantum Computing Inc. gained amid renewed retail and institutional interest in the quantum computing sector. The stock remains highly volatile with elevated short interest, and recent momentum has been fueled by broader enthusiasm for next-generation computing technologies and government-backed quantum research initiatives.

stocks.name: Micron Technology
stocks.explanation: Micron rallied ahead of its Q3 FY2026 earnings on June 24, with expectations running high as the global memory shortage intensifies. The company’s HBM chips are sold out for all of 2026, and AI-driven demand for both DRAM and NAND far exceeds supply. Apple CEO Tim Cook’s comments about ‘unsustainable’ memory costs further validated Micron’s pricing power.

stocks.name: KLA Corporation
stocks.explanation: KLA Corporation rose with the broader semiconductor equipment sector as AI infrastructure spending continues to drive demand for advanced chip inspection and metrology tools. FERC’s new orders to fast-track data center grid connections also boosted sentiment for companies that supply the upstream semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem.

stocks.name: Monolithic Power Systems
stocks.explanation: Monolithic Power Systems gained as demand for its power management ICs accelerates alongside the AI data center buildout. The company’s chips are critical for power delivery in GPU-dense server racks, and the ongoing expansion of AI training and inference infrastructure is driving strong order growth across its product portfolio.
